title: SΔϕ-44 - Path Principles as Meta-Governance Axioms
package_title: SΔϕ-44 - Path Principles as Meta-Governance Axioms: Computational Non-Viability, Restoration Cost, and Structural Path Closure AI-Readable Package
creator: Sofience
type: AI-readable package / working paper / path-principles meta-governance audit module
core_claim: A path does not close because it is forbidden. A path closes when continuing through it becomes computationally non-viable, restoration-costly, structurally sealed, or unavailable relative to alternative routes.

primary_formulae.operational: Operational Path Closure = Computational Non-Viability + Exponential Transition Cost + Restoration-Cost Burden + Structural Sealing + Available Route Collapse
primary_formulae.maxim: Prohibition closes entrances. Cost and structure close paths.

do_not_use_as: proof that policy existence equals governance success
proof that enforcement equals structural sealing
proof that safety rule equals path closure
proof that blocked entrance equals impossible route
proof that high friction equals closure
